task.prompt: When a strict-mode sub-router is mounted and a request arrives without the expected trailing slash, Express incorrectly allows the route to match. The trim_prefix function in the router strips the mount path from the URL, and when the remaining URL is empty, it rewrites it to '/' to ensure a leading slash. This synthetic slash fools the strict sub-router into matching a '/' route even though the original request had no trailing slash. No error is thrown — the route simply matches when it should not. Investigate how the router strips mount paths and how the slash rewriting interacts with strict routing in sub-routers.

hint_levels.symptom_only: A strict-mode router's route for '/' matches requests that do not have a trailing slash on the mount path. Routes that should reject these requests silently accept them instead.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in lib/router/index.js in the trim_prefix function at lines 280-296. When stripping the mount path leaves an empty string, the code rewrites req.url to '/' and sets slashAdded=true. This synthetic slash is then seen by a strict sub-router as a legitimate trailing slash. The interaction with fast_slash in lib/router/layer.js line match() amplifies the bug.
hint_levels.function_hint: In lib/router/index.js trim_prefix at line 283, when req.url[0] !== '/' the code sets req.url = '/' + req.url and slashAdded=true. For an empty URL this creates '/' which matches the strict sub-router. In lib/router/layer.js match(), fast_slash=true (set when path==='/' with end=false) causes any path to match unconditionally. The fix requires not applying the slashAdded rewrite when the outer router is strict.

reasoning_chain: Read symptom: strict sub-router '/' route matches requests without trailing slash on mount path
Open lib/router/index.js and find proto.handle() then trace to trim_prefix at lines 280-296
Find slashAdded branch: when req.url[0] !== '/' it rewrites to '/' + req.url — for empty string this produces '/'
Open lib/router/layer.js and find Layer constructor — fast_slash = path === '/' && opts.end === false
Find Layer.prototype.match — when fast_slash is true, any path matches unconditionally
Open lib/router/route.js and find dispatch() — strict route pattern for '/' matches the synthetic '/' url
Open lib/application.js and find how strict option flows through lazyrouter() and app.use()
Realize: slashAdded rewrite bypasses strict routing by creating a synthetic '/' that fast_slash treats as a match
Apply fix: guard slashAdded rewrite so it does not fire when outer router uses strict mode, or make fast_slash honor strict flag
